title: Disney Q1 FY26 Earnings: Executive Commentary
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Disney's Q1 FY26 earnings report highlights strong performance, with CEO Bob Iger and CFO Hugh Johnston noting record box office revenue and ongoing theme park
excerpt: Disney's Q1 FY26 earnings report highlights strong performance, with CEO Bob Iger and CFO Hugh Johnston noting record box office revenue and ongoing theme park expansions. ESPN's strong ratings and continued success were also emphasized.

content.introduction: Disney's Q1 FY26 earnings report highlights strong performance, with CEO Bob Iger and CFO Hugh Johnston noting record box office revenue and ongoing theme park expansions. ESPN's strong ratings and continued success were also emphasized.
body: ## Walt Disney Company (The). Intraday chart for Walt Disney Company (The). Stock Walt Disney Company (The). The Walt Disney Company reported its FY26 first quarter results on Monday with CEO Bob Iger and CFO Hugh Johnston focusing on the company's recent accomplishments. "We are pleased with our start to fiscal 2026, and our achievements reflect the tremendous progress we have made," Iger and Johnston said in an executive commentary on Monday. In addition, ESPN demonstrated its leadership as the preeminent destination for sports with strong ratings in the quarter, and Disney's Experiences segment has expansion projects underway at every one of the company's theme parks. Disney's film studios generated more than $6.5 billion at the global box office in calendar year 2025, making this its third biggest year ever and its ninth year as No. 1 at the global box office in the last 10 years, Iger and Johnston noted.

We are pleased with our start to fiscal 2026, and our achievements reflect the tremendous progress we have made, Iger and Johnston said in an executive commentary on Monday. In addition, ESPN demonstrated its leadership as the preeminent destination for sports with strong ratings in the quarter, and Disneys Experiences segment has expansion projects underway at every one of the companys theme parks. These achievements reflect the progress weve made over the past three years to fortify the company, position our businesses for future growth over the long-term, and set Disney on a path for continued success, the two said. Disneys film studios generated more than $6.5 billion at the global box office in calendar year 2025, making this its third biggest year ever and its ninth year as No. 1 at the global box office in the last 10 years, Iger and Johnston noted.

Title: The Walt Disney Company Publishes Transcript of Q1 FY26 Earnings Results Conference Call
The Walt Disney Company Publishes Transcript of Q1 FY26 Earnings Results Conference Call. Walt Disney Company -0.40%. | Walt Disney Company  DIS | 105.58 | -0.40% |. The Walt Disney Company has published the official transcript of its First Quarter 2026 Financial Results Conference Call. The event was attended by Chief Executive Officer Bob Iger, Senior Executive Vice President & Chief Financial Officer Hugh Johnston, and Executive Vice President, Investor Relations and Treasurer Carlos Gómez. During the call, Disney’s management discussed the company’s financial performance for Q1 FY26, provided insight into recent business decisions, and addressed future business plans. And we're able to keep attendance rates high from that perspective.” The team also addressed changes in segment disclosure and how these changes align with Disney’s long-term vision for managing and communicating about the Entertainment segment. “Our press release, Form 10-Q, and management's posted prepared remarks were issued earlier this morning and are available on our website,” stated the Executive Vice President, Investor Relations and Treasury, emphasizing transparency and access to information for investors.
